title: Request Smuggling, Response Splitting, Parser Differentials, and Downgrades · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection.
Reject or consistently handle bare line endings, whitespace before colons, obsolete folding, leading blank lines, and invalid request-line separators across every hop.
Compare raw and decoded targets, path separators, percent encodings, authority, query boundaries, and routing between policy and downstream recipients.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate
steps.instruction:
Order the Downgrade Consistency Gate evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Downgrade Consistency Gate assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate, keep Downgrade Consistency Gate connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A debug protocol team handles case 1781 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Downgrade Consistency Gate has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Downgrade Consistency Gate precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Order the regression proof · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug protocol team handles case 1781 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.

For Order the regression proof · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A safe HTTP/2 front end guarantees the translated HTTP/1.1 backend request cannot be ambiguous.

options.text: Declare inputs, result and error contracts, ownership, and every goroutine stop condition for the debug Downgrade Consistency Gate case
Trace types, copies, aliases, deferred work, blocking points, and synchronization before editing for the debug Downgrade Consistency Gate case
Run the smallest isolated Go program and retain output or invariant evidence for the debug Downgrade Consistency Gate case
Change a boundary input or schedule, verify cleanup and races, then state toolchain limits for the debug Downgrade Consistency Gate case

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-downgrade-consistency-gate-l3oh3k" that uses case 5448 to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Downgrade Consistency Gate: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug protocol team handles case 1789 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate.
steps.why: Retrieving Downgrade Consistency Gate through the concrete multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate, keep Downgrade Consistency Gate connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Downgrade Consistency Gate precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug protocol team handles case 1789 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Downgrade Consistency Gate, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A safe HTTP/2 front end guarantees the translated HTTP/1.1 backend request cannot be ambiguous.
